---
title: "scRNAseq Data Analysis Pipeline - MV"
output: html_document
date: "2026-02-13"
---

Load Libraries

```{r}
install.packages("tidyverse")
install.packages("viridis")
library(viridis)
library(CellChat)
library(tidyverse)
library(Seurat)
library(ComplexHeatmap)
library(patchwork)
library(SingleR)
```

Set Input Path

```{r}
path <- "/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/iawards/single cell/abu/abu onedrive contents/Bioinformatics/scRNAseq/outs/"
setwd(path)
```

```{r}
list.files(path)
set.seed(42) # For reproducibility 
```

# Import Data ————————————————————————————

Seurat (Read10X) function reads the folder containing the 'matrix', 'features' and 'barcodes' files and loads them all together to create one object for analysis.

-   Matrix = sparse expression counts (genes x cells)

-   Features = gene info (names, IDs, feature type)

-   Barcodes = cell identifiers (one per column in matrix)

```{r}
# Read three files from first folder and stores as sparse matrix "a17"
a17 <- Read10X(
  "~/Desktop/iawards/single cell/abu/abu onedrive contents/Bioinformatics/scRNAseq/outs/DUY679A17") 
a17

# Removes barcoes and adds sample identifier prefix "a17"
barcodes <- colnames(a17)
new_barcodes <- paste0("A17_", barcodes)
colnames(a17) <- new_barcodes

# Create seurat object using QC filters 
a17 <- CreateSeuratObject(counts = a17, min.cells = 3, min.features = 200)
a17

a18 <- Read10X(
  "~/Desktop/iawards/single cell/abu/abu onedrive contents/Bioinformatics/scRNAseq/outs/DUY679A18")
a18
barcodes <- colnames(a18)
new_barcodes <- paste0("A18_", barcodes)
colnames(a18) <- new_barcodes
a18 <- CreateSeuratObject(counts = a18, min.cells = 3, min.features = 200)
a18


a21 <- Read10X(
  "~/Desktop/iawards/single cell/abu/abu onedrive contents/Bioinformatics/scRNAseq/outs/DUY679A21")
a21
barcodes <- colnames(a21)
new_barcodes <- paste0("A21_", barcodes)
colnames(a21) <- new_barcodes
a21 <- CreateSeuratObject(counts = a21, min.cells = 3, min.features = 200)
a21

a22 <- Read10X(
  "~/Desktop/iawards/single cell/abu/abu onedrive contents/Bioinformatics/scRNAseq/outs/DUY679A22")
a22
barcodes <- colnames(a22)
new_barcodes <- paste0("A22_", barcodes)
colnames(a22) <- new_barcodes
a22 <- CreateSeuratObject(counts = a22, min.cells = 3, min.features = 200)
a22

a37 <- Read10X(
  "~/Desktop/iawards/single cell/abu/abu onedrive contents/Bioinformatics/scRNAseq/outs/DUY679A37")
a37
barcodes <- colnames(a37)
new_barcodes <- paste0("A37_", barcodes)
colnames(a37) <- new_barcodes
a37 <- CreateSeuratObject(counts = a37, min.cells = 3, min.features = 200)
a37

a38 <- Read10X(
  "~/Desktop/iawards/single cell/abu/abu onedrive contents/Bioinformatics/scRNAseq/outs/DUY679A38")
a38
barcodes <- colnames(a38)
new_barcodes <- paste0("A38_", barcodes)
colnames(a38) <- new_barcodes
a38 <- CreateSeuratObject(counts = a38, min.cells = 3, min.features = 200)
a38
```

Exploring Objects

```{r}
slotNames(a17)
slotNames(a17@meta.data)
```

# Quality Control ————————————————————————————-

## Basic filtering

Remove cells with very low or very high gene/total molecule count because these indicate low quality or empty cells and doublets or multiplets. Also remove cells with high percentage (higher than 5-10%) of mitochondrial genes (these indicate low quality/dying cells).

In the seurat object metadata (after this block runs), we will have nCount_RNA, nFeature_RNA and percent_mt:

-   nCount_RNA = total number of molecules/genes detected in each cell (high number (\>50,000-100,000) of counts could suggest the cell is a doublet or multiplet)

-   nFeature_RNA = total number of unique genes detected in each cell (low number (\<500-100) suggests the cell is dead or empty droplet)

I used threshold of 5% for mitchondrial genes because this is the standard metric

A17:

```{r}
#### Calulate percentage of features with certain condition #####

# In the a17 seurat object, look for % of mitochondrial genes and store this info in the metadata of the a17 object 
a17[['percent_mt']] <- PercentageFeatureSet(object = a17, pattern = "^MT-")
a17@meta.data

# Visualise the metadata columns 
a17_metadata <- VlnPlot(a17, features =c("nCount_RNA", "nFeature_RNA", "percent_mt"), ncol = 3)
#ggsave("/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/a17_metadata.png", plot = a17_metadata, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

a17_metadata_scatter <- FeatureScatter(a17, feature1 = "nCount_RNA", feature2 = "nFeature_RNA") + geom_smooth(method = "lm")
#ggsave("/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/a17_metadata_scatter.png", plot = a17_metadata_scatter, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

#Check size of a17 before filtering (19931  5932)
a17_size_prefilt <- dim(a17)

# Filter out poor quality cells
# Filtering out cells that have unique feature counts
# And filter out cells with high % of mitochondrial genes 
a17_filtered <- subset(a17, subset = 
              nFeature_RNA > 500 &      # Remove very low-quality cells
              nFeature_RNA < 6000 &     # Remove upper outliers
                
              nCount_RNA > 1000 &       # Remove cells with very few counts
              nCount_RNA < 50000 &      # Remove extreme count outliers (doublets etc)
              percent_mt < 5)           # Remove high mitochondrial % 

a17_size_postfilt <- dim(a17_filtered)
a17_size_postfilt #19931  3561
a17_filtered_scatter <- FeatureScatter(a17_filtered, feature1 = "nCount_RNA", feature2 = "nFeature_RNA") + geom_smooth(method = "lm")
#ggsave("/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/a17_filtered_scatter.png", plot = a17_filtered_scatter, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)



```

A18:

```{r}
#### Calulate percentage of features with certain condition #####

# In the a18 seurat object, look for % of mitochondrial genes and store this info in the metadata of the a18 object 
a18[['percent_mt']] <- PercentageFeatureSet(object = a18, pattern = "^MT-")
a18@meta.data

# Visualise the metadata columns 
a18_metadata <- VlnPlot(a18, features =c("nCount_RNA", "nFeature_RNA", "percent_mt"), ncol = 3)
#ggsave("/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/a18_metadata.png", plot = a18_metadata, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

a18_metadata_scatter <- FeatureScatter(a18, feature1 = "nCount_RNA", feature2 = "nFeature_RNA") + geom_smooth(method = "lm")
#ggsave("/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/a18_metadata_scatter.png", plot = a18_metadata_scatter, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

#Check size of a18 before filtering 
a18_size_prefilt <- dim(a18)
a18_size_prefilt

# Filter out poor quality cells
# Filtering out cells that have unique feature counts 
# And filter out cells with high % of mitochondrial genes 
a18_filtered <- subset(a18, subset = 
              nFeature_RNA > 500 &      # Remove very low-quality cells
              nFeature_RNA < 6000 &     # Remove potential doublets (upper outliers)
              nCount_RNA > 1000 &       # Remove cells with very few counts
              nCount_RNA < 50000 &      # Remove extreme count outliers
              percent_mt < 5)           # Remove high mitochondrial % 

a18_size_postfilt <- dim(a18_filtered)
a18_size_postfilt 
a18_filtered_scatter <- FeatureScatter(a18_filtered, feature1 = "nCount_RNA", feature2 = "nFeature_RNA") + geom_smooth(method = "lm")
#ggsave("/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/a18_filtered_scatter.png", plot = a18_filtered_scatter, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

```

A21:

```{r}
#### Calulate percentage of features with certain condition #####

# In the a21 seurat object, look for % of mitochondrial genes and store this info in the metadata of the a21 object 
a21[['percent_mt']] <- PercentageFeatureSet(object = a21, pattern = "^MT-")
a21@meta.data

# Visualise the metadata columns 
a21_metadata <- VlnPlot(a21, features =c("nCount_RNA", "nFeature_RNA", "percent_mt"), ncol = 3)
#ggsave("/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/a21_metadata.png", plot = a21_metadata, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

a21_metadata_scatter <- FeatureScatter(a21, feature1 = "nCount_RNA", feature2 = "nFeature_RNA") + geom_smooth(method = "lm")
#ggsave("/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/a21_metadata_scatter.png", plot = a21_metadata_scatter, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

#Check size of a21 before filtering 
a21_size_prefilt <- dim(a21)
a21_size_prefilt

# Filter out poor quality cells
# Filtering out cells that have unique feature counts 
# And filter out cells with high % of mitochondrial genes 
a21_filtered <- subset(a21, subset = 
              nFeature_RNA > 500 &      # Remove very low-quality cells
              nFeature_RNA < 6000 &     # Remove potential doublets (upper outliers)
              nCount_RNA > 1000 &       # Remove cells with very few counts
              nCount_RNA < 50000 &      # Remove extreme count outliers
              percent_mt < 5)           # Remove high mitochondrial % 

a21_size_postfilt <- dim(a21_filtered)
a21_size_postfilt 
a21_filtered_scatter <- FeatureScatter(a21_filtered, feature1 = "nCount_RNA", feature2 = "nFeature_RNA") + geom_smooth(method = "lm")
#ggsave("/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/a21_filtered_scatter.png", plot = a21_filtered_scatter, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

```

A22:

```{r}
#### Calulate percentage of features with certain condition #####

# In the a22 seurat object, look for % of mitochondrial genes and store this info in the metadata of the a22 object 
a22[['percent_mt']] <- PercentageFeatureSet(object = a22, pattern = "^MT-")
a22@meta.data

# Visualise the metadata columns 
a22_metadata <- VlnPlot(a22, features =c("nCount_RNA", "nFeature_RNA", "percent_mt"), ncol = 3)
#ggsave("/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/a22_metadata.png", plot = a22_metadata, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

a22_metadata_scatter <- FeatureScatter(a22, feature1 = "nCount_RNA", feature2 = "nFeature_RNA") + geom_smooth(method = "lm")
#ggsave("/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/a22_metadata_scatter.png", plot = a22_metadata_scatter, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

#Check size of a22 before filtering 
a22_size_prefilt <- dim(a22)
a22_size_prefilt

# Filter out poor quality cells
# Filtering out cells that have unique feature counts 
# And filter out cells with high % of mitochondrial genes 
a22_filtered <- subset(a22, subset = 
              nFeature_RNA > 500 &      # Remove very low-quality cells
              nFeature_RNA < 6000 &     # Remove potential doublets (upper outliers)
              nCount_RNA > 1000 &       # Remove cells with very few counts
              nCount_RNA < 50000 &      # Remove extreme count outliers
              percent_mt < 5)           # Remove high mitochondrial % 

a22_size_postfilt <- dim(a22_filtered)
a22_size_postfilt 
a22_filtered_scatter <- FeatureScatter(a22_filtered, feature1 = "nCount_RNA", feature2 = "nFeature_RNA") + geom_smooth(method = "lm")
#ggsave("/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/a22_filtered_scatter.png", plot = a22_filtered_scatter, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

```

A37:

```{r}
#### Calulate percentage of features with certain condition #####

# In the a37 seurat object, look for % of mitochondrial genes and store this info in the metadata of the a37 object 
a37[['percent_mt']] <- PercentageFeatureSet(object = a37, pattern = "^MT-")
a37@meta.data

# Visualise the metadata columns 
a37_metadata <- VlnPlot(a37, features =c("nCount_RNA", "nFeature_RNA", "percent_mt"), ncol = 3)
#ggsave("/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/a37_metadata.png", plot = a37_metadata, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

a37_metadata_scatter <- FeatureScatter(a37, feature1 = "nCount_RNA", feature2 = "nFeature_RNA") + geom_smooth(method = "lm")
#ggsave("/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/a37_metadata_scatter.png", plot = a37_metadata_scatter, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

#Check size of a37 before filtering 
a37_size_prefilt <- dim(a37)
a37_size_prefilt

# Filter out poor quality cells
# Filtering out cells that have unique feature counts 
# And filter out cells with high % of mitochondrial genes 
a37_filtered <- subset(a37, subset = 
              nFeature_RNA > 500 &      # Remove very low-quality cells
              nFeature_RNA < 6000 &     # Remove potential doublets (upper outliers)
              nCount_RNA > 1000 &       # Remove cells with very few counts
              nCount_RNA < 50000 &      # Remove extreme count outliers
              percent_mt < 5)           # Remove high mitochondrial % 

a37_size_postfilt <- dim(a37_filtered)
a37_size_postfilt 
a37_filtered_scatter <- FeatureScatter(a37_filtered, feature1 = "nCount_RNA", feature2 = "nFeature_RNA") + geom_smooth(method = "lm")
#ggsave("/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/a37_filtered_scatter.png", plot = a37_filtered_scatter, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

```

A38:

```{r}
#### Calulate percentage of features with certain condition #####

# In the a38 seurat object, look for % of mitochondrial genes and store this info in the metadata of the a38 object 
a38[['percent_mt']] <- PercentageFeatureSet(object = a38, pattern = "^MT-")
a38@meta.data

# Visualise the metadata columns 
a38_metadata <- VlnPlot(a38, features =c("nCount_RNA", "nFeature_RNA", "percent_mt"), ncol = 3)
#ggsave("/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/a38_metadata.png", plot = a38_metadata, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

a38_metadata_scatter <- FeatureScatter(a38, feature1 = "nCount_RNA", feature2 = "nFeature_RNA") + geom_smooth(method = "lm")
#ggsave("/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/a38_metadata_scatter.png", plot = a38_metadata_scatter, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

#Check size of a38 before filtering 
a38_size_prefilt <- dim(a38)
a38_size_prefilt

# Filter out poor quality cells
# Filtering out cells that have unique feature counts 
# And filter out cells with high % of mitochondrial genes 
a38_filtered <- subset(a38, subset = 
              nFeature_RNA > 500 &      # Remove very low-quality cells
              nFeature_RNA < 6000 &     # Remove potential doublets (upper outliers)
              nCount_RNA > 1000 &       # Remove cells with very few counts
              nCount_RNA < 50000 &      # Remove extreme count outliers
              percent_mt < 5)           # Remove high mitochondrial % 

a38_size_postfilt <- dim(a38_filtered)
a38_size_postfilt 
a38_filtered_scatter <- FeatureScatter(a38_filtered, feature1 = "nCount_RNA", feature2 = "nFeature_RNA") + geom_smooth(method = "lm")
#ggsave("/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/a38_filtered_scatter.png", plot = a38_filtered_scatter, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

```

## Normalise Data --------------------------------

Global scaling normalisation method normalises the feature expression measurement for each cell by the total expression (multiples by scale factor 10,000 and log transforms the result)

This log normalisation method divides each cell's gene counts by that cell's total counts, multiplies by 10,000 (to put cells on a common scale), then log transforms. This accounts for differences in sequencing depth between cells while keeping all the data.

```{r}
a17_norm_filt <- NormalizeData(a17_filtered)
str(a17_norm_filt) # Normalised values are stored in $ data

a18_norm_filt <- NormalizeData(a18_filtered)
str(a18_norm_filt) 

a21_norm_filt <- NormalizeData(a21_filtered)
str(a21_norm_filt) 

a22_norm_filt <- NormalizeData(a22_filtered)
str(a22_norm_filt) 

a37_norm_filt <- NormalizeData(a37_filtered)
str(a37_norm_filt) 

a38_norm_filt <- NormalizeData(a38_filtered)
str(a38_norm_filt) 
```

## Identifying Highly Variable Features ----------------------

Calculate subset of features (genes) that have high cell-to-cell variation in the dataset (so high expression in some cells and low expression in others)

Highly variable genes can help highlight biological signals in sc datasets (the important data that helps you distinguish between subset of cells)

Finding highly variable genes for each sample and plotting them

```{r}
#sample a17
a17_norm_filt <- FindVariableFeatures(a17_norm_filt, selection.method = 'vst', nFeatures = 2000)

# Top 10 highly variable genes
a17_top10 <- head(VariableFeatures(a17_norm_filt), 10)
a17_top10_plot <- LabelPoints(plot = VariableFeaturePlot(a17_norm_filt), points = a17_top10, repel = TRUE) + ggtitle("Top 10 Variable Genes - A17")

#ggsave("/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/Variable_Genes/a17_top10_genes.png", plot = a17_top10_plot, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

#sample a18
a18_norm_filt <- FindVariableFeatures(a18_norm_filt, selection.method = 'vst', nFeatures = 2000)

# Top 10 highly Variable_Genes
a18_top10 <- head(VariableFeatures(a18_norm_filt), 10)
a18_top10_plot <- LabelPoints(plot = VariableFeaturePlot(a18_norm_filt), points = a18_top10, repel = TRUE) + ggtitle("Top 10 Variable Genes - A18")


#ggsave("/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/Variable_Genes/a18_top10_genes.png", plot = a18_top10_plot, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

#sample a21
a21_norm_filt <- FindVariableFeatures(a21_norm_filt, selection.method = 'vst', nFeatures = 2000)

# Top 10 highly variable genes
a21_top10 <- head(VariableFeatures(a21_norm_filt), 10)
a21_top10_plot <- LabelPoints(plot = VariableFeaturePlot(a21_norm_filt), points = a21_top10, repel = TRUE) + ggtitle("Top 10 Variable Genes - A21")


#ggsave("/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/Variable Genes/a21_top10_genes.png", plot = a21_top10_plot, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

#sample a22
a22_norm_filt <- FindVariableFeatures(a22_norm_filt, selection.method = 'vst', nFeatures = 2000)

# Top 10 highly variable genes
a22_top10 <- head(VariableFeatures(a22_norm_filt), 10)
a22_top10_plot <- LabelPoints(plot = VariableFeaturePlot(a22_norm_filt), points = a22_top10, repel = TRUE) + ggtitle("Top 10 Variable Genes - A22")


#ggsave("/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/Variable_Genes/a22_top10_genes.png", plot = a22_top10_plot, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

#sample a37
a37_norm_filt <- FindVariableFeatures(a37_norm_filt, selection.method = 'vst', nFeatures = 2000)

# Top 10 highly variable genes
a37_top10 <- head(VariableFeatures(a37_norm_filt), 10)
a37_top10_plot <- LabelPoints(plot = VariableFeaturePlot(a37_norm_filt), points = a37_top10, repel = TRUE) + ggtitle("Top 10 Variable Genes - A37")


#ggsave("/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/Variable Genes/a37_top10_genes.png", plot = a37_top10_plot, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

#sample a38
a38_norm_filt <- FindVariableFeatures(a38_norm_filt, selection.method = 'vst', nFeatures = 2000)

# Top 10 highly variable genes
a38_top10 <- head(VariableFeatures(a38_norm_filt), 10)
a38_top10_plot <- LabelPoints(plot = VariableFeaturePlot(a38_norm_filt), points = a38_top10, repel = TRUE) + ggtitle("Top 10 Variable Genes - A38")


#ggsave("/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/Variable Genes/a38_top10_genes.png", plot = a38_top10_plot, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

```

Comparing all and top 10 highly variable genes across samples Creates: - CSV comparison table of which genes appear in which samples - Heatmap visualises this comparison - UpSet plot = similar to venn diagram but better for this amount (6) of samples

Firstly for all HVGs...

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# Create a named list of HVGs per sample
hvg_list <- list(
  A17 = VariableFeatures(a17_norm_filt),
  A18 = VariableFeatures(a18_norm_filt),
  A21 = VariableFeatures(a21_norm_filt),
  A22 = VariableFeatures(a22_norm_filt),
  A37 = VariableFeatures(a37_norm_filt),
  A38 = VariableFeatures(a38_norm_filt)
)

# Summary table 
# Shows which genes appear in which samples (1 = present, 0 = absent)
all_genes <- unique(unlist(hvg_list))

presence_table <- data.frame(
  Gene = all_genes,
  sapply(hvg_list, function(x) as.integer(all_genes %in% x))
)

# Add a column counting how many samples each gene appears in
presence_table$N_Samples <- rowSums(presence_table[, -1])
presence_table <- presence_table[order(-presence_table$N_Samples), ]

print(presence_table)
#write.csv(presence_table, "/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/Variable Genes/HVG_comparison_table.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Heatmap of presence/absence of HVGs present in 2 or more samples
presence_table_filtered <- presence_table[presence_table$N_Samples >= 2, ]

# Converts data from wide format (one column per sample) to long format (one row per gene-sample combination)
presence_long <- presence_table_filtered %>%
  dplyr::select(-N_Samples) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = -Gene, names_to = "Sample", values_to = "Present")

heatmap_plot <- ggplot(presence_long, aes(x = Sample, y = Gene, fill = factor(Present))) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("0" = "lightgrey", "1" = "steelblue"),
                    labels = c("Absent", "Present"),
                    name = "") +
  ggtitle("Highly Variable Gene Overlap Across Samples") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

#ggsave("/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/Variable Genes/HVG_heatmap.png", 
       #plot = heatmap_plot, width = 8, height = 10, dpi = 300)

#  UpSet plot (shows combinations of overlap) 
#install.packages("UpSetR")
library(UpSetR)

upset_data <- as.data.frame(sapply(hvg_list, function(x) as.integer(all_genes %in% x)))
rownames(upset_data) <- all_genes

graphics.off()  # close all devices cleanly
#png("/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/Variable Genes/HVG_upset.png",
   # width = 10, height = 6, units = "in", res = 300)
#upset(upset_data, sets = names(hvg_list), order.by = "freq", main.bar.color = "steelblue")
dev.off()
```

Then for just top 10 HVGs in each sample...

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# Create a named list of top 10 HVGs per sample
top10_list <- list(
  A17 = a17_top10,
  A18 = a18_top10,
  A21 = a21_top10,
  A22 = a22_top10,
  A37 = a37_top10,
  A38 = a38_top10
)

# --- 1. Summary table ---
# Shows which genes appear in which samples (1 = present, 0 = absent)
top10all_genes <- unique(unlist(top10_list))

top10presence_table <- data.frame(
  Gene = top10all_genes,
  sapply(top10_list, function(x) as.integer(top10all_genes %in% x))
)

# Add a column counting how many samples each gene appears in
top10presence_table$N_Samples <- rowSums(top10presence_table[, -1])
top10presence_table <- top10presence_table[order(-top10presence_table$N_Samples), ]

print(top10presence_table)
#write.csv(top10presence_table, "/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/Variable_Genes/Top10HVG_comparison_table.csv", row.names = FALSE)

#  Heatmap of presence/absence ---
top10presence_long <- top10presence_table %>%
  dplyr::select(-N_Samples) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(-Gene, names_to = "Sample", values_to = "Present")

top10heatmap_plot <- ggplot(top10presence_long, aes(x = Sample, y = Gene, fill = factor(Present))) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("0" = "lightgrey", "1" = "steelblue"),
                    labels = c("Absent", "Present"),
                    name = "") +
  ggtitle("Top 10 HVG Overlap Across Samples") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

#ggsave("/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/Variable_Genes/Top10HVG_heatmap.png", 
     #  plot = top10heatmap_plot, width = 8, height = 10, dpi = 300)

# --- 3. UpSet plot (shows combinations of overlap) ---
library(UpSetR)

top10upset_data <- as.data.frame(sapply(top10_list, function(x) as.integer(top10all_genes %in% x)))
rownames(top10upset_data) <- top10all_genes

#png("/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/Variable_Genes/Top10HVG_upset.png",
  #  width = 10, height = 6, units = "in", res = 300)
#upset(top10upset_data, sets = names(top10_list), order.by = "freq", main.bar.color = "steelblue")
dev.off()
```

# Scaling --------------------------- -

Removes unwanted sources of variation like batch effects or biological variation (like cell cycle stage or mitochrondrial contamination). This ensures that the clusters are due to actual differences in gene expression and not other effects.

For each sample, taking the 2000 HVGs identified earlier and scaling them so each gene has a mean of 0 and variance of 1 across all cells - puts all genes on same scale so that the highly expressed genes don't dominate downstream steps (e.g.PCA)

```{r}
a17_norm_filt <- ScaleData(a17_norm_filt, features = VariableFeatures(a17_norm_filt))
View(a17_norm_filt@assays$RNA) # in here is counts for all genes and cells (dgCMatrix), the normalised data (dgCMatrix), and scale.data (scaled data for only the 2000 HVGs as dense matrix )

a18_norm_filt <- ScaleData(a18_norm_filt, features = VariableFeatures(a18_norm_filt))
View(a18_norm_filt@assays$RNA)

a21_norm_filt <- ScaleData(a21_norm_filt, features = VariableFeatures(a21_norm_filt))
View(a21_norm_filt@assays$RNA)

a22_norm_filt <- ScaleData(a22_norm_filt, features = VariableFeatures(a22_norm_filt))
View(a22_norm_filt@assays$RNA)

a37_norm_filt <- ScaleData(a37_norm_filt, features = VariableFeatures(a37_norm_filt))
View(a37_norm_filt@assays$RNA)

a38_norm_filt <- ScaleData(a38_norm_filt, features = VariableFeatures(a38_norm_filt))
View(a38_norm_filt@assays$RNA)
```

# Merging Samples -------------------

Integrate samples using canonical correlation analysis. This aligns shared biological structure across patients, while reducing batch/patient-driven clustering and preserving cell-type signal.

**Harmony** - Takes the PCA embedding and iteratively adjusts it to remove batch effects while preserving biological variation. It's generally faster and more memory efficient than CCA.

```{r}
#install.packages("harmony")
library(harmony)
```

```{r}
# Merge all samples into one object 
all_merged <- merge(
  x = a17_norm_filt,
  y = list(a18_norm_filt, a21_norm_filt,
           a22_norm_filt, a37_norm_filt, a38_norm_filt)
)

# Join layers, normalise, find variable features, scale
DefaultAssay(all_merged) <- "RNA"
all_merged <- JoinLayers(all_merged)
all_merged <- NormalizeData(all_merged)
all_merged <- FindVariableFeatures(all_merged, nfeatures = 2000)
# Explicitly pass variable features to ScaleData
var_features <- VariableFeatures(all_merged)
all_merged <- ScaleData(all_merged, features = var_features)

saveRDS(all_merged, "all_merged_integrated.rds")

```

# Dimensionality Reduction --------------------------

Visualise the multidimensional dataset

## PCA -----------------

PCA = summarising all gene expression data across many genes into principal components

Genes with large loadings for the first PCS explain most of the variability in the dataset.

-   positive loadings = genes are posiviely correlated with that PC

-   negative loadings = genes are negetively correlated with that PC

Cells are clustered based on their gene expression profile (the basis of differentiation), so different clusters usually represent different cell types.

```{r}
# Run PCA
all_merged <- RunPCA(all_merged, npcs = 30, features = var_features)

# Visualise PCA - elbow plot helps decide how many PCs to use downstream
elbow_plot <- ElbowPlot(all_merged, ndims = 30) + ggtitle("Level of Variance in Each Principal Component - Merged Samples")
ggsave("/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/PCA/Harmony/elbow_plot.png",
       plot = elbow_plot, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)

# Visualise which genes drive each PC
print(all_merged[["pca"]], dims = 1:5, nfeatures = 5)

# Extract top genes for each PC and save
pc_loadings <- as.data.frame(Loadings(all_merged, reduction = "pca"))
pc_top_genes <- apply(pc_loadings, 2, function(x) names(sort(abs(x), decreasing = TRUE)[1:10]))
write.csv(pc_top_genes, "/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/PCA/Harmony/PC_top_genes.csv")


# Run Harmony to correct for batch effects
# group.by.vars tells it which metadata column contains the batch info
all_merged <- RunHarmony(all_merged, group.by.vars = "orig.ident")

# Use Harmony embedding for downstream analysis instead of PCA
all_merged <- FindNeighbors(all_merged, reduction = "harmony", dims = 1:15) # Used top 15 PCs, based on where elbow plot plataeus 
all_merged <- FindClusters(all_merged, resolution = c(0.1, 0.2, 0.3, 0.5, 0.7, 1)) # Trying different resolution parameters

```

## UMAP ——————————-

Another dimensionality reduction method

Non-linear

```{r}

# --- 1. Setup Environment ---
# Define output directory to avoid repeating long strings
out_dir <- "/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/PCA/Harmony/"

# --- 2. Pre-Correction Diagnostics (PCA) ---
# These show the data before batch correction
PC1_Heatmap <- DimHeatmap(all_merged, dims = 1, cells = 500, balanced = TRUE)
PCA_DimPlot <- DimPlot(all_merged, reduction = "pca") + NoLegend() + ggtitle("Standard PCA")
PCA_DimPlot
ggsave(paste0(out_dir, "PC1_Heatmap.png"), plot = PC1_Heatmap, width = 8, height = 6)
ggsave(paste0(out_dir, "PCA_DimPlot.png"), plot = PCA_DimPlot, width = 8, height = 6)

# --- 3. Run Harmony & UMAP ---
# run UMAP using the batch-corrected Harmony embeddings
all_merged <- RunUMAP(all_merged, reduction = "harmony", dims = 1:15)

# Diagnostic: View Harmony embeddings (Check for batch overlap)
Harmony_DimPlot <- DimPlot(all_merged, reduction = "harmony") + NoLegend() + ggtitle("Harmony Embeddings")
Harmony_DimPlot
ggsave(paste0(out_dir, "Harmony_DimPlot.png"), plot = Harmony_DimPlot, width = 8, height = 6)

# Diagnostic: Check batch mixing on UMAP
Batch_mixing <- DimPlot(all_merged, reduction = "umap", group.by = "orig.ident") + ggtitle("Batch Mixing (UMAP)")
Batch_mixing
ggsave(paste0(out_dir, "Batch_Mixing.png"), plot = Batch_mixing, width = 8, height = 6)

# --- 4. Final Clustering (Resolution 0.2) ---
# Set the active identity to preferred resolution
Idents(all_merged) <- 'RNA_snn_res.0.2'

# Plot 0.2 Clusters on UMAP (Final figure style)
UMAP_0.2 <- DimPlot(all_merged, reduction = "umap", label = TRUE) + 
  ggtitle("UMAP Clusters: Res 0.2")
UMAP_0.2

# Plot 0.2 Clusters on Harmony
Harmony_res_0.2 <- DimPlot(all_merged, reduction = "harmony", label = TRUE) + 
  ggtitle("Harmony Clusters: Res 0.2")
Harmony_res_0.2

# Save final cluster plots
ggsave(paste0(out_dir, "UMAP_res.0.2.png"), plot = UMAP_0.2, width = 8, height = 6)
ggsave(paste0(out_dir, "Harmony_res.0.2.png"), plot = Harmony_res_0.2, width = 8, height = 6)


```

# Finding Markers —————————————

Find marker genes in each cluster to identify what the clusters are.

Marker genes are highly and specifically expression in one cluster compared to others, used to identtify what cell type each cluster is.

**avg_log2FC** - how highly expressed it is in that cluster vs others (higher = more specific)

**p_val_adj** - adjusted p-value (want this \< 0.05)

```{r}
# --- 1. Ensure Resolution is 0.2 ---
Idents(all_merged) <- 'RNA_snn_res.0.2'
DefaultAssay(all_merged) <- "RNA" # Markers should always be found on the RNA counts

saveRDS(all_merged, "/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/all_merged.rds")
# --- 2. Run Marker Discovery ---
markers <- FindAllMarkers(
  all_merged,
  only.pos = TRUE, # gives only positive markers (genes that are upregulated)
  min.pct = 0.25,  # gene must be detected in at least 25% of cells in either the cluster being tested or the rest of the cells 
  logfc.threshold = 0.25 # gene must have a minimum 0.25 log2 fold change difference between the cluster and all other cells
)

# Filter for significance and high expression
markers_filtered <- markers %>%
  filter(p_val_adj < 0.05, pct.1 > 0.25)

# Get the top 10 gene NAMES (as a vector) for plotting
top_10_names <- markers_filtered %>%
  group_by(cluster) %>%
  slice_max(avg_log2FC, n = 10) %>%
  pull(gene) %>%             # Extract just the gene column
  unique()                   # Remove duplicates if a gene marks multiple clusters

# --- 3. Save the Data ---
out_dir <- "/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/Marker_Genes/Harmony/"
dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

write.csv(markers_filtered, paste0(out_dir, "markers_res0.2_all.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

# --- 4. Plotting ---

# A. Canonical Markers 
canonical_markers <- c("MKI67", "PTPRC", "EPCAM", "COL1A1", "PECAM1", "KRT19")
canonical_plot <- FeaturePlot(all_merged, features = canonical_markers, ncol = 3)
canonical_plot

# B. Top Markers from Data 
# Using a subset of top_10_names so the plot isn't too crowded
dataset_markers_plot <- FeaturePlot(all_merged, features = head(top_10_names, 9), ncol = 3)
dataset_markers_plot
# C. Violin Plot for Clusters
cluster_vln <- VlnPlot(all_merged, features = head(top_10_names, 6), pt.size = 0)
cluster_vln
# Save Plots
ggsave(paste0(out_dir, "canonical_markers.png"), plot = canonical_plot, width = 12, height = 8)
ggsave(paste0(out_dir, "top_dataset_markers.png"), plot = dataset_markers_plot, width = 12, height = 8)
ggsave(paste0(out_dir, "cluster_vln.png"), plot = cluster_vln, width = 12, height = 8)
```

## Annotating Clusters Using SingleR

```{r}
if (!require("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))
    install.packages("BiocManager")

BiocManager::install("SingleR")
BiocManager::install("celldex") # Reference datasets
```

```{r}
library(SingleR)
library(celldex)

# skin reference
ref <- celldex::HumanPrimaryCellAtlasData()

# Expression matrix
expr <- LayerData(all_merged, layer = "data") # normalised data

# Cluster IDs
clusters <- all_merged$seurat_clusters

#Run SingleR at cluster level
singler_cluster <- SingleR(
  test = expr,
  ref = ref,
  labels = ref$label.main,
  clusters = clusters
)

# Create mapping table
cluster_map <- data.frame(
  cluster = levels(as.factor(clusters)),
  label = singler_cluster$labels
)

# Attach labels to each cell
all_merged$SingleR_annotation <- 
  cluster_map$label[
    match(
      as.character(all_merged$seurat_clusters),
      cluster_map$cluster
    )
  ]

# Check
table(all_merged$SingleR_annotation, useNA = "ifany")

# Visualise annotated clusters
annotated_clusters <- DimPlot(all_merged,
        group.by = "SingleR_annotation",
        label = TRUE,
        repel = TRUE,
        pt.size = 0.3) +
  ggtitle("SingleR Annotated Clusters")

ggsave("/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/PCA/Harmony/Annotated_SingleR_UMAP.png",
       plot = annotated_clusters,
       width = 8,
       height = 6,
       dpi = 300)

# After SingleR annotation
saveRDS(all_merged, "all_merged_annotated.rds")
```

I ran annotation through the HPC using singleR, using a specific Atopic Dermatitis reference atlas from the Single Cell Portal.

I first ran it using the celltype_coarse (16 categories) metadata from the reference, but now I will rerun using celltype_granular (86 categories) to get more detail on the annotations because the coarse run gave the same annotations as the immune reference.

Here is the new annotation using singleR through the HPC with the granular reference **Skin Cell Atlas (SCP2738), Vorstandlechner et al. (2024)** - "A multi-modal census of the human skin" published in ***Nature Communications***.

```{r}
# Load seurat object with SingleR labels 
all_merged <- readRDS("~/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/SingleR_Annotations/query_annotated.rds")

# -----------UMAP CLusters-----------
# Check the labels
table(all_merged$SingleR.labels)

# Visualise on UMAP
SingleR_UMAP <- DimPlot(all_merged, group.by = "SingleR.labels", label = TRUE, repel = TRUE, label.size = 6) + ggtitle("SingleR Annotations Using Skin Cell Atlas (SCP2738)") + theme(axis.title = element_text(size = 20),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 18),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 22))

SingleR_UMAP
ggsave("~/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/SingleR_Annotations/SingleR_UMAP.png", plot=SingleR_UMAP, width=12, height=8, dpi=300)


# Now just looking at keratinocytes to see distrinctions
# Define custom colours — KCs in distinct colours, others kept neutral
custom_colours <- c(
  "KC 1" = "#FF0000",        # red
  "KC 2" = "#FF8C00",        # orange
  "KC 3" = "#FFD700",        # gold
  "KC 4" = "#00CED1",        # dark turquoise
  "KC 5" = "#9400D3",        # purple
  "KC cycling" = "#FF69B4",  # pink
  "Cornified KC 1" = "#8B0000",  # dark red
  "Cornified KC 2" = "#A0522D",  # brown
  # keep everything else grey to focus on KCs
  "B Naive/Mem" = "grey70",
  "CD8+ CTL" = "grey70",
  "DC 1" = "grey70",
  "DC 2" = "grey70",
  "DC 2 CD83+" = "grey70",
  "DC 2 cycling" = "grey70",
  "DC MMP12+" = "grey70",
  "HF" = "grey70",
  "ILC" = "grey70",
  "ILC cycling" = "grey70",
  "LDC" = "grey70",
  "Melano IFI27 hi" = "grey70",
  "Melano S100A4-" = "grey70",
  "Melano S100A4+" = "grey70",
  "mmDC" = "grey70",
  "NK" = "grey70",
  "Plasmablast" = "grey70",
  "Sebaceous gland" = "grey70",
  "Sweat gland 1" = "grey70",
  "Sweat gland 2" = "grey70",
  "T CREM hi" = "grey70",
  "T CREM lo FOS lo" = "grey70",
  "T FOS hi" = "grey70",
  "T FOS hi cycling" = "grey70",
  "Treg" = "grey70",
  "Treg cycling" = "grey70",
  "γδ T" = "grey70",
  "NA" = "grey90"
)

# Get UMAP coordinates with cell type labels
umap_df <- as.data.frame(all_merged@reductions$umap@cell.embeddings)
umap_df$celltype <- all_merged$SingleR.labels

# Calculate centroid positions for KC labels only
kc_labels <- c("KC 1", "KC 2", "KC 3", "KC 4", "KC 5", "KC cycling", "Cornified KC 1", "Cornified KC 2")

label_positions <- umap_df %>%
  filter(celltype %in% kc_labels) %>%
  group_by(celltype) %>%
  summarise(umap_1 = median(umap_1), umap_2 = median(umap_2))

# Plot without any labels
KCs <- DimPlot(all_merged,
             group.by = "SingleR.labels",
             label = FALSE,
             cols = custom_colours) +
  ggtitle("Keratinocyte Subtypes") +
  geom_text(data = label_positions,
             aes(x = umap_1, y = umap_2, label = celltype),
             size = 5) + theme(axis.title = element_text(size = 20),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 18),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 22))

KCs

ggsave("/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/SingleR_Annotations/Keratinocytes_umap.png",
       plot = KCs, width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 300)



# ---------------Cell Type Distirbutions------------

# Check quality
pred <- readRDS("~/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/SingleR_Annotations/singler_predictions.rds")
plotScoreHeatmap(pred)
plotDeltaDistribution(pred)
# Check how many NA cells there are
sum(is.na(all_merged$SingleR.labels))

# Cell type distribution
# 1. Bar plot of cell type counts
Cell_Type_Counts <- ggplot(all_merged@meta.data, aes(x = reorder(SingleR.labels, SingleR.labels, function(x) -length(x)), fill = SingleR.labels)) +
  geom_bar() +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(title = "Cell Type Distribution Using Skin Cell Atlas (SCP2738)", x = "Cell Type", y = "Number of Cells") +
  NoLegend()


ggsave("~/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/SingleR_Annotations/Cell_Tpe_Counts.png", plot=Cell_Type_Counts, width=12, height=8, dpi=300)

# 2. Cell type proportions per sample
Cells_per_Sample <- ggplot(all_merged@meta.data, aes(x = orig.ident, fill = SingleR.labels)) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1), axis.title = element_text(size = 20),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 18),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 22)) +
  labs(title = "Cell Type Proportions per Sample", x = "Sample", y = "Proportion")
# Specifically keratinocytes defined by colour
KCs_per_Sample <- ggplot(all_merged@meta.data, aes(x = orig.ident, fill = SingleR.labels)) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colours) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 18),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 22)) +
  labs(title = "Keratinocyte Proportions per Sample", x = "Sample", y = "Proportion")

ggsave("~/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/SingleR_Annotations/Keratinocytes_Sample.png", plot=KCs_per_Sample, width=12, height=8, dpi=300)

ggsave("~/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/SingleR_Annotations/Cells_per_Sample.png", plot=Cells_per_Sample, width=12, height=8, dpi=300)

# 3. Simple table of counts and percentages
df <- as.data.frame(table(all_merged$SingleR.labels))
colnames(df) <- c("CellType", "Count")
df$Percentage <- round(df$Count/sum(df$Count)*100, 2)
df <- df[order(-df$Count),]
df
write.csv(df, "/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/SingleR_Annotations/cell_type_distribution.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```

## Finding the Most Highly Expressed Genes per Cluster —-

The **log2FC** from the markers file is effect size measurement

Plotting the effects size of the top genes in each cluster, taking into account the pct.1 (% of cells in that cluster expressing that gene)

```{r}

# Check they match
unique(markers$cluster)
unique(cluster_annotation_map$seurat_clusters)

cluster_annotation_map <- unique(
  all_merged@meta.data[, c("seurat_clusters", "SingleR_annotation")]
)
cluster_annotation_map$seurat_clusters <- as.character(cluster_annotation_map$seurat_clusters)
markers$cluster <- as.character(markers$cluster)

# Top 5 genes per cluster
top5 <- markers %>%
  left_join(cluster_annotation_map, by = c("cluster" = "seurat_clusters")) %>%
  group_by(SingleR_annotation) %>%
  slice_max(avg_log2FC, n = 5)

# Bubble plot
Effect_per_Cluster <- ggplot(top5,
       aes(x = SingleR_annotation,
           y = gene,
           size = pct.1,
           color = avg_log2FC)) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "lightblue", high = "darkblue") +
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(1, 8)) +
  labs(x = "Cell Type", y = "Gene", color = "log2FC", size = "% cells expressing") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

Effect_per_Cluster

ggsave("/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/PCA/Harmony/Effect_per_cell_type.png",
       plot = Effect_per_Cluster,
       width = 8,
       height = 8,
       dpi = 300)

top5_clusters <- markers %>%
  left_join(cluster_annotation_map, by = c("cluster" = "seurat_clusters")) %>%
  group_by(SingleR_annotation) %>%
  slice_max(avg_log2FC, n = 5, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  arrange(SingleR_annotation, cluster)

# Order genes by cluster so they appear grouped on y-axis
top5_clusters$gene <- factor(top5$gene, levels = unique(top5$gene))

Effect_per_Cluster_ordered <- ggplot(top5_clusters,
       aes(x = SingleR_annotation,
           y = gene,
           size = pct.1,
           color = avg_log2FC)) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "lightblue", high = "darkblue") +
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(1, 8)) +
  labs(x = "Cell Type", y = "Gene", color = "log2FC", size = "% cells expressing") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

Effect_per_Cluster_ordered
ggsave("/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/PCA/Harmony/Effect_per_cell_type_ordered.png",
       plot = Effect_per_Cluster_ordered,
       width = 8,
       height = 8,
       dpi = 300)
```

### Heatmap

```{r}
# Get top 5 genes per cluster (using cluster numbers, not cell type labels)
top5_heatmap <- markers %>%
  group_by(cluster) %>%
  slice_max(avg_log2FC, n = 5, with_ties = FALSE)

# Set identities to SingleR annotation for labelling
Idents(all_merged) <- all_merged$SingleR_annotation

all_merged <- ScaleData(all_merged, features = top5_heatmap$gene)

# Plot heatmap
heatmap_plot <- DoHeatmap(
  all_merged,
  features = top5_heatmap$gene,
  label = TRUE,
  size = 3
) + scale_fill_gradient2(low = "blue", mid = "white", high = "red")

heatmap_plot

ggsave("Marker_Genes/Harmony/Top_genes_heatmap.png",
       plot = heatmap_plot,
       width = 14,
       height = 10,
       dpi = 300)
```

# Cellchat —————————

CellChat infers cell-cell communication

```{r}
devtools::install_github("jinworks/CellChat")
install.packages("devtools")
devtools::install_github("thomasp85/patchwork")
if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))
    install.packages("BiocManager")

BiocManager::install("ComplexHeatmap")

devtools::install_github('immunogenomics/presto')


```

```{r}
library(presto)

all_merged <- readRDS("all_merged_annotated.rds")

# Step 1: Create CellChat object
# Use normalised counts and SingleR annotations
cellchat <- createCellChat(
  object = all_merged,
  group.by = "SingleR_annotation",
  assay = "RNA"
)

# Step 2: Set the ligand-receptor database (human)
CellChatDB <- CellChatDB.human
cellchat@DB <- CellChatDB

# Step 3: Subset to signalling genes only
cellchat <- subsetData(cellchat)

# Step 4: Identify overexpressed genes and interactions
cellchat <- identifyOverExpressedGenes(cellchat)
cellchat <- identifyOverExpressedInteractions(cellchat)

# Step 5: Compute communication probabilities
cellchat <- computeCommunProb(cellchat)
cellchat <- filterCommunication(cellchat, min.cells = 10)

# Step 6: Infer communication at pathway level
cellchat <- computeCommunProbPathway(cellchat)

# Step 7: Aggregate communication network
cellchat <- aggregateNet(cellchat)

# Step 8: Visualise - circle plot
groupSize <- as.numeric(table(cellchat@idents))

par(mfrow = c(1, 2))
circle_count <- as.ggplot(function() 
  netVisual_circle(cellchat@net$count,
                   vertex.weight = groupSize,
                   weight.scale = TRUE,
                   label.edge = FALSE,
                   title.name = "Number of interactions"))

circle_weight <- as.ggplot(function() 
  netVisual_circle(cellchat@net$weight,
                   vertex.weight = groupSize,
                   weight.scale = TRUE,
                   label.edge = FALSE,
                   title.name = "Interaction strength"))

ggsave("/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/CellChat/Interaction_Number.png",
       plot = circle_count,
       width = 8, height = 8, dpi = 300)

ggsave("/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/CellChat/Interaction_Strength.png",
       plot = circle_weight,
       width = 8, height = 8, dpi = 300)

# Bubble plot of all communications
netVisual_bubble(cellchat,
                 sources.use = NULL,
                 targets.use = NULL,
                 remove.isolate = FALSE)

# Heatmap of interactions
install.packages("ggplotify")
library(ggplotify)

heatmap_count <- as.ggplot(function() 
  ComplexHeatmap::draw(netVisual_heatmap(cellchat, measure = "count")))

heatmap_weight <- as.ggplot(function() 
  ComplexHeatmap::draw(netVisual_heatmap(cellchat, measure = "weight")))

ggsave("/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/CellChat/Heatmap_Count.png",
       plot = heatmap_count,
       width = 8, height = 8, dpi = 300)

ggsave("/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/CellChat/Heatmap_Weight.png",
       plot = heatmap_weight,
       width = 8, height = 8, dpi = 300)

# Top pathways
# Compute centrality scores
cellchat <- netAnalysis_computeCentrality(cellchat)

# Rank signalling pathways by strength
gg <- rankNet(cellchat, mode = "single")
gg

ggsave("/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/Research Project/Rerunning Pipelines/My_Pipeline/CellChat/Ranked_Signalling.png",
       plot = gg,
       width = 8,
       height = 10,
       dpi = 300)
```

```{r}
ncol(all_merged)
```
